description: This figure presents the calculated pseudobinary phase diagram of the Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C–N system, computed using Thermo-Calc software with the TCFE8 database. The vertical axis shows temperature ranging from approximately 700 K to 1800 K, while the horizontal axis represents nitrogen mass fraction from 0.0 to 2.0 wt%. The diagram delineates multiple phase fields including liquid (L), ferrite (delta), austenite (gamma), alpha-phase, and various precipitates such as AlN, M5C2, and M23C6. Key phase regions identified include L+AlN, L+delta+AlN, delta+gamma+AlN, gamma+AlN, gamma+AlN+M5C2, and delta+alpha+AlN+M23C6, among others. As nitrogen content increases, the austenite phase field significantly expands toward higher temperatures and broader composition ranges, while ferrite gradually diminishes and eventually disappears. AlN remains stable across a wide temperature and composition range. The diagram supports the experimental observation that nitrogen acts as a strong austenite stabilizer, promotes austenite nucleation, and suppresses ferrite growth, thereby explaining the reduced ferrite content and refined grain structure observed in high-nitrogen low-density steel.
